claims.claim: LIBs with lithium-nickel-cobalt-aluminium (NCA) cathode chemistry had a laminar burning velocity range between 0.2 m/s to 1.1 m/s, which is higher than typical hydrocarbons.
claims.quote: LIBs with lithium-nickel-cobalt-aluminium (NCA) cathode chemistry had a higher LBV than typical hydrocarbons, with an LBV range between 0.2 m/s to 1.1 m/s.

claims.claim: DNV reported an explosion pressure between 90 kPa (gauge) and 270 kPa (gauge) in a partly confined geometry with various Li-ion vent gas release profiles.
claims.quote: DNV reported an explosion pressure between 90 kPa (gauge) and 270 kPa (gauge) in a partly confined geometry, with various Li-ion vent gas release profiles.

claims.claim: The maximum explosion pressure in the 1-m channel experiments was 1.36 kPa (gauge) at equivalence ratio of 1.08, while the CFD simulation predicted 1.45 kPa (gauge) at equivalence ratio of 1.0.
claims.quote: the maximum explosion pressure was 1.36 kPa (gauge), at φ equal to 1.08; whereas, the maximum explosion pressure for the simulation was 1.45 kPa (gauge), at φ equal to 1.0.

claims.claim: At 650 K, the Gülder equation underpredicts LBVs on the lean and rich side with a standard deviation of error of 338 mm/s and R² of 0.66.
claims.quote: At 650 K, the SDE is 338 mm/s, and R2 is 0.66.

claims.claim: Pressure and flame front velocity oscillations that occurred as the flame approached the channel opening were not accurately predicted by the CFD simulations.
claims.quote: pressure and flame front velocity oscillations, which occurred as the flame approached the channel opening, were not accurately predicted.
